metadata.title: Delete Old LinkedIn Projects (Technical Jargon Versions)

reasoning.problem: LinkedIn profile has 10 projects total: 5 old (technical jargon) + 5 new (HR-approved domain names). Recipe replay partially executed before system crash, creating NEW projects instead of updating OLD ones.
why_delete: Old projects use technical jargon (STILLWATER OS, SOLACEAGI, PZIP, PHUCNET, IF-THEORY)
New projects use domain names (Stillwater.com, SolaceAgi.com, PZip.com, IFTheory.com, Phuc.net)
New projects have HR-approved copy (outcome-focused, who benefits, impact bullets)
Duplicates confuse profile visitors and dilute messaging
LinkedIn best practices: 5-7 projects max, all with clear value props
reasoning.strategy: Navigate to projects page → Find old project by name → Click edit → Click delete → Confirm → Repeat for all 5
success_criteria: All projects use domain name format (*.com)
reasoning.llm_learnings: When LinkedIn recipe replay is interrupted, it may create duplicates instead of updating existing entries. Always verify final state and create cleanup recipes. For future: add transaction-like behavior to recipes (check if exists → update vs create).
research_applied: LinkedIn Project Section Guide 2026 - Max 5-7 projects, focus on outcomes
Resume Writing Lab - Use 'who benefits' language, not technical specs
Teal HQ - Impact bullets with metrics, domain authority signals
Resume Worded - Professional copy, avoid jargon, clear value props

projects_to_delete.name: STILLWATER OS
projects_to_delete.reason: Technical jargon, replaced by Stillwater.com with HR copy
projects_to_delete.old_description: Universal compression platform + persistent intelligence OS. 65537D OMEGA architecture. 4.075x compression ratio.

projects_to_delete.name: SOLACEAGI
projects_to_delete.reason: All caps, technical jargon, replaced by SolaceAgi.com
projects_to_delete.old_description: Verified AI Expert Council (65537 decision-makers, not chatbot). Proof-based reasoning, persistent identity, CPU-native.

projects_to_delete.name: PZIP
projects_to_delete.reason: All caps, replaced by PZip.com with impact bullets
projects_to_delete.old_description: Compression product. Beats LZMA on all file types. 91.4% win rate. Commercial-ready, open source.

projects_to_delete.name: PHUCNET
projects_to_delete.reason: All caps, replaced by Phuc.net with clearer positioning
projects_to_delete.old_description: Solo founder brand hub. Ecosystem center for all 5 projects. Support via tips (ko-fi.com/phucnet). Building in public.

projects_to_delete.name: IF-THEORY
projects_to_delete.reason: Hyphenated caps, replaced by IFTheory.com with research focus
projects_to_delete.old_description: Mathematical foundations. 137 prime discoveries. Fermat primes, Mersenne numbers, Sophie Germain primes. Physics + mathematics R&D.

execution_trace.reasoning: Start at projects page to see all current projects

execution_trace.note: Manual deletion required - LinkedIn uses dynamic IDs that change per session
execution_trace.reasoning: Automated clicking would require complex element identification. Manual is faster (2-3 min) and more reliable.

execution_trace.note: For each old project (STILLWATER OS, SOLACEAGI, PZIP, PHUCNET, IF-THEORY):
substeps: Click pencil/edit icon (usually top-right of card)
Wait for edit modal to open

execution_trace.reasoning: Refresh page to verify all deletions completed

execution_trace.note: Verify final state: Only 5 projects with domain names (Stillwater.com, SolaceAgi.com, PZip.com, IFTheory.com, Phuc.net)

next_ai_instructions: This recipe documents WHY we delete (reasoning) but LinkedIn's dynamic UI prevents full automation. For future: (1) Use this recipe as a checklist during manual deletion, (2) Consider LinkedIn API for programmatic deletion if available, (3) Create transaction-like recipe behavior (if exists → update, else → create) to prevent duplicates.

cpu_replay_instructions.note: Currently requires manual execution of deletion steps. Recipe provides checklist and reasoning.

what_i_learned: Recipe replay can create duplicates if interrupted mid-execution
LinkedIn uses dynamic IDs - full automation is brittle
Manual deletion (2-3 min) often beats debugging selectors (30+ min)
Externalized reasoning in recipes = $0 LLM cost for future runs
Recipes serve as both automation AND documentation
next_time: Add pre-flight check: if project exists, update vs create
Add rollback mechanism for interrupted recipes
Consider LinkedIn API for reliable project management
Add evidence snapshots at every step for debugging
